{{Quote|We've been cut off by Neimoidian support staff. Hey! You Neimoidians! Are you bein' paid enough to get ''SHOT''?|Captain Orsai makes a status report during his escape from [[Spacestation 1138|Space Station 1138]]|<ref name="JSF3">[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter#Mission 3: Prison Break|Jedi Starfighter: Mission 3: Prison Break]]</ref>}}
'''Captain Orsai''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] member of the [[Mere Resistance]] movement led by [[Sol Sixxa]] and [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
===Prison Break===
[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Orsai and his troops were captured by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and incarcerated in the infamous [[Spacestation 1138|Space Station 1138]]. While they were being held, [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] managed to [[Raid on the Trade Federation base|acquire]] the codes to the space station to free Orsai and his men and have access to weapons to escape the station. When the pirate forces arrived a the space station, the [[Liberator (Mere)|Liberator]], a [[Mere troop transport|Mere Troop Transport]] piloted by [[Kole]] would dock the station once the defense [[Laser turret/Legends|laser turrets]] were destroyed. Nym helped [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] destroy the turrets. While that was going, however, the codes that Nym used to get Orsai free accidentally activated a squad of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|Battle Droids]], pinning down Orsai and his men. Nym managed to help them get pass them by destroying an [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] that Orsai asked for him to destroy. When Orsai and his men got to the lift they were unable to use it so they took the stairs but not before being cut of by [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] support staff, who didn't put up much resistance. By the time the prisoners reached the escape hatch, the ''Liberator'' docked with the station and Orsai and his men managed to get onboard as the ''Liberator'' escaped into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] under escort from friendly fighters.

===Assault on Trade Federation Sensor Station===
After freeing the prisoners, the Mere resistance planned on going on the offensive. Orsai would then pilot one of two [[Mere demolition boat|Demolition Boats]], the other being piloted by [[Sol Sixxa]], that were filled with explosives to destroy the [[Trade Federation Sensor Station]]. Despite various obstacles such as [[Trade Federation submarine]]s, [[Autoturret/Legends|autoturrets]], [[Tractor beam/Legends|Tractor Beam Towers]] and [[Mine/Legends|mines]] both ships managed to reach the Sensor Station managed to get inside and destroy the Sensor Station.

===Battle of Nod Kartha===
Captain Orsai later took command of the [[Mere cruiser]] ''[[Kethor]]'' during the [[Battle of Nod Kartha|assault]] on [[Nod Kartha]], but his cruiser was damaged by fire from one of the [[Orbital Defense Cannon]]s. The hull was compromised and the hyperdrive engines were disabled as a result. Later on in the heated battle Orsai's men fled the doomed ship in the [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], while Orsai, despite protests from his allies, remained onboard to guide the crippled ''Kethor'' into the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]], and perished in the resulting explosion. Orsai's sacrifice ensured the fall of Nod Kartha's defense grid, and later, the destruction of the groundside [[trihexalon]] factory.

==Personality and traits==
Despite only being human, Orsai was welcomed into the Mere Resistance and was a loyal fighter. After he escaped from prison, he went on to fight with distinction. He was unafraid to die for a cause he believed in.

==Behind the scenes==
Orsai was voiced by [[Roger Jackson]] in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''. Jackson also voiced various other units in the game, and provided the voices for many other Star Wars video game characters.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'' {{1st}}
